sections.paragraph:
προθυμία, ας, ἡ (πρόθυμος; Hom. et al.; ins, pap; Sir 45:23; TestSol; TestJob 11:1; EpArist, Philo, Joseph.) exceptional interest in being of service, willingness, readiness, goodwill 2 Cor 8:19; 9:2; Dg 1. W. ἐκτένεια 1 Cl 33:1; ἐν ἀγαθῇ πρ. 2:3. μετὰ πάσης προθυμίας (as Hdt. 4, 98; Pla., Rep. 412e; Philo, Abr. 246; Jos., Ant. 15, 124; in ins esp. of benefactors SIG 532, 6f; IMagnMai 97, 74; IGerasaWelles 192; OGI 229, 98; 669, 13 μετὰ προθυμίας) Ac 17:11 here w. emphasis on goodwill and absence of prejudice (FDanker, NTS 10, ’64, 366f). εἰ ἡ πρ. πρόκειται if willingness is present, i.e. if a person is willing 2 Cor 8:12. προθυμίαν ἔχειν have zeal (Hdt. et al.) Hs 5, 3, 4b; foll. by gen. for someth. 5, 3, 4a. ἡ προθυμία τοῦ θέλειν (as Pla., Leg. 3, 697d) 2 Cor 8:11.—Larfeld I 499. DELG s.v. θυμός. M-M. TW. Spicq.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

esp. esp. = especially

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

IMagnMai IMagnMai = Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander—Lists 3

IGerasaWelles IGerasaWelles = “The Inscriptions”, ed. CWelles—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Hs Hs = Similitudes

foll. foll. = followed, following

gen. gen. = genitive

someth. someth. = something

Larfeld Larfeld = WL., Handbuch der griechischen Epigraphik—Lists 3, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 870
